other_observers: Larry Semo; Joe Roller identified the bird
specific_location: On eastern half of the northern beach at Jackson Reservoir
description: A smallish and lanky jaeger, the bird was overall medium-dark brown, with a strong gray cast. The face and crown were gray-brown, contrasting with a paler, unstreaked nape; the throat was also slightly paler. The rest of the underparts were uniform in color, except for the undertail coverts, which were strongly barred in black and white, with the bars extending across (from side to side) of that feather tract. The uppertail coverts were quite similar in pattern and coloration to the undertail coverts. The back feathers, scapulars, and upperside wing coverts were all tipped and/or fringed with a pale color that varied from very pale buff to grayish-white depending upon the lighting.  The black primaries were only barely tipped with whitish, not the pattern of buff- or rufous-fringed tips typical of Parasitic Jaeger. The secondaries contrasted strongly with with the upperwing coverts forming a distinct black trailing edge when in flight. The upperwing virtually lacked a white flash at the base of the primaries -- I could just detect a hint of white there. I could not count the number of white primary shafts on the bird, but could certainly tell that it was a relatively small number (<5).  The underwing coverts were tipped/fringed with whitish and the axillar region was barred with black and white. The underside of the outermost two primaries were nearly all dark, creating a dark leading edge to the pale patch created by the paler middle primaries. The rectrices were black and the R1s extended beyond the others a distance about equal to 1/6 to 1/8 of the length of the R2s. The R1s came to a quite rounded point, not the sharp point typical of Parasitic Jaeger.  The bill was fairly short (about equal in length to the distance between the bill base and the eye), relatively thick, and with the distal 40-45% being black, contrasting with the pale base of indeterminate color.Behaviors: Most of the time that the bird was in view, it was walking/running amongst the smartweed (Polygonum sp.) chasing grasshoppers and other insects. We saw it fly once.Call: none heardPlumage: Juvenal
